policy_statement: Portugal committed (as an EU Member State) to mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries by 2030.
verbatim_text: As an EU Member State, Portugal made the commitment to mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries (LDC) by 2030.
section_header: 4.5. Provision of new and additional resources
extraction_rationale: Quantified commitment with deadline (by 2030) regarding ODA to LDCs.
reasoning: This statement is an actionable finance commitment with a clear, measurable target (0.15%–0.20% of GNI as ODA to LDCs) and an explicit deadline (by 2030). It is spatially specific in terms of beneficiary scope (Least Developed Countries). However, it does not indicate a binding domestic mechanism (e.g., law/mandate), so it relies on political commitment language, though the presence of hard numbers and a deadline supports validity under the strict rule.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text specifies a measurable outcome: "mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries (LDC)". Timeline: The text includes an explicit deadline: "by 2030." Spatial specificity: The policy applies to a defined recipient group/location: "Least Developed Countries (LDC)." Binding mechanism: No enforceable instrument is cited (no law/mandate/ordinance), only "made the commitment" as an EU Member State. Language strength: Strong numeric commitment is present with a deadline ("0.15% to 0.20%" and "by 2030"), supporting auditable delivery. Weak language: No terms like "encourage/explore/aim" appear; it states "made the commitment" with hard numbers.
